mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months tightens financial conditions and raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as valuation multiples compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS rose to 2.33 in Aug 2022 vintage from near-zero in early 2022
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 in absolute value over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-return frequency in rising-rate periods exceeds the unconditional 65.5% base rate.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above the 3.0% threshold, monetary policy transitions from accommodative to restrictive territory relative to historical neutral-rate estimates, triggering a repricing of long-duration equities and depressing S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 3.0% for the first time in the cycle
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.33 in Aug 2022 approaching the 3.0% threshold amid rapid tightening
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following the 3.0% crossing are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional 60-day returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.2 percentage points from its cyclical low signals an inflection in labor market strength that historically precedes broader economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases more than 0.2pp over 3 months from its cyclical low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6-3.7% near cycle lows as of Aug 2022
failure_condition: Following UNRATE increases above the 0.2pp threshold, 60-day forward SPX returns remain positive in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of elevated CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth and rising FEDFUNDS indicates the Fed is actively fighting inflation with real rates increasing, producing short-horizon risk-off equity behavior over the next week as markets price additional tightening surprises.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 6% while FEDFUNDS is simultaneously rising month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised value 295.097 in Aug 2022 vintage remains near multi-decade highs alongside FEDFUNDS at 2.33%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns during joint high-inflation/rising-rate windows are not significantly more negative than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or positive-return frequency exceeds 60%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST during a monetary tightening cycle signals deteriorating interest-rate-sensitive demand that spreads to broader consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter, but only when FEDFUNDS is simultaneously rising.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS rises
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised value 1381 thousand units in Jul 2022 down from vintage 1446, indicating housing sector cooling
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns after HOUST declines during tightening regimes are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect disappears when tested outside tightening regimes (falsifying regime-dependence claim).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial capacity utilization and corporate output, which translates into stronger forward earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised value 101.0963 in Aug 2022 vintage down from 104.5464, showing recent softening
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO trailing 3-month growth and forward 20d SPX returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive INDPRO growth periods do not outperform negative growth periods at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in PAYEMS monthly growth below 150,000 net new jobs signals cooling labor demand consistent with an economy responding to tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as growth expectations are downgraded.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly net change falls below 150,000
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised value 153,362 thousand in Aug 2022 vintage still reflecting robust job growth above threshold
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following sub-150k payroll prints are positive in more than 55% of historical instances since 2005, or effect is not significant at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of decelerating RSAFS retail sales growth and rising UNRATE jointly signals a weakening consumer demand base that consumer-driven equity valuations have not yet priced in, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth turns negative while UNRATE rises simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised value 663,566 million in Aug 2022 vintage down from 683,291 vintage estimate, indicating softer retail spending
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during joint RSAFS decline/UNRATE rise episodes are not significantly more negative than unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or positive-return frequency exceeds 55%.
